constraints.special: Binary classification head - very large K

baseline_kernel:
// Binary classifier: large feature vector K=16K
// Standard f16: 16K × 2B = 32KB per vector
analysis: Binary features: K=16K stored as 16K bits = 2KB
schedule_reasoning: Huge K-tile (1024 bits) for binary data
K=16384 processed in 16 iterations
Massive storage/bandwidth savings
Used in: binary hash codes, locality-sensitive hashing
concept: Binary GEMM large-K - binary feature matching

constraints.special: ResNet final conv - tiny spatial, huge channels

baseline_kernel:
// Final ResNet layer: 7×7 spatial, 2048 channels
// Almost all compute is channel mixing
analysis: M dimension of implicit GEMM is tiny
schedule_reasoning: Tiny M tile (32) for small spatial
Wide K tile (64) for massive K dimension
5 stages for deep K pipelining
Final layer before global average pool

constraints.special: HERK upper fill - different from lower

baseline_kernel:
// HERK upper fill: store upper triangle
// C[i,j] for i <= j physically stored
analysis: Lower triangle = conjugate of upper: C[i,j] = conj(C[j,i])
Different memory access pattern than lower fill
schedule_reasoning: Upper fill selected for LAPACK compatibility
Some libraries expect upper-triangular output
Tile scheduler only computes upper region
Used in: LAPACK-compatible interfaces

constraints.special: HEMM left side - Hermitian A × B

baseline_kernel:
// Left side: C = A × B where A is Hermitian
// A is N×N=2048², B is N×M=2048×4096
analysis: A stored as lower triangle, upper via conjugate
schedule_reasoning: 64×128 tile: balanced M×N output
Left side HEMM common in physics
Lower fill: lower triangle stored physically
Used in: quantum operator multiplication
concept: HEMM left side - Hermitian operator × matrix

task.data_type: int8 input → int32 accumulate → f16 output

baseline_kernel:
// INT8 GEMM → INT32 → f16 output
// Quantized compute, fp16 output for next layer
schedule_reasoning: Dequantization fused in epilogue: int32 × scale → f16
No intermediate int32 storage
f16 output feeds into f16 layers
Used in: mixed-precision inference pipelines
concept: Mixed precision int8→f16 - quantized with dequant

baseline_kernel:
// Dilation=8: 3×3 filter covers 17×17 receptive field
// Very sparse sampling of input
schedule_reasoning: kStrided iterator handles dilation=8
Filter offsets multiplied by 8
Large receptive field for global context
Used in: dense prediction, scene parsing

baseline_kernel:
// 1×1 conv: 64→256 channel expansion
// First layer of ResNet bottleneck
analysis: Pure channel projection - no spatial filter
schedule_reasoning: Standard 128×128 tile for balanced C→K mapping
1×1 filter: implicit GEMM is exactly a GEMM
K dimension = C (no R×S multiplier)
ResNet bottleneck first projection

baseline_kernel:
// 1×1 conv: 256→64 channel reduction
// Last layer of ResNet bottleneck
analysis: Bottleneck last layer: compress channels back down
schedule_reasoning: 128×64 tile: wide C (256), narrow K (64)
Asymmetric tile for asymmetric channel mapping
K < C: compression layer
ResNet bottleneck final projection
